CheckTitle: AI Agent Assume-Role Sessions Must Not Exceed 3600 Seconds
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Identity**. Verifies that the maximum session duration for cross-account or assumed-role sessions is set to 3600 seconds (one hour) or less. Shorter session durations limit the window of opportunity for exploiting stolen session tokens during agent-to-cloud or cross-account operations.
Risk: An agent with an unlimited or very long session duration produces long-lived temporary credentials (STS tokens, Azure access tokens). If such a token is exfiltrated — through prompt injection, SSRF, or a compromised environment variable — the attacker gains extended, unrevocable cloud access.

Code.Other:
1. On AWS: Set the MaxSessionDuration on the IAM role to 3600 seconds.
2. On Azure: Use Managed Identity or configure the token lifetime policy on the service principal to 1 hour.
3. On GCP: Use short-lived service account tokens (max 1 hour is the platform default).
4. Add trust policy conditions (aws:RequestedRegion, aws:PrincipalTag) to further restrict assume-role usage.

Recommendation.Text: Set the maximum session duration for all agent assumed-role sessions to 3600 seconds or less. Add conditional trust policy constraints to restrict when and from where the role can be assumed.

Notes: ASPM-010 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
